evals.prompt: We're launching a new B2B SaaS product for design teams in 6 weeks. It's a design review tool. We have a small audience (500 email subscribers, 2k Twitter followers). Help us plan the launch.
evals.expected_output: Should read .agents/product-marketing-context.md if present. Should CLASSIFY the launch type first (new-product, full launch) before emitting tactics. Should fill the ORB inventory gate with the real numbers given (Owned: email list 500; Rented: Twitter 2k; Borrowed: to be identified) and flag any unknowns rather than inventing. Should fork the five phases scaled to the 6-week horizon (Internal, Alpha, Beta, Early Access, Full) with T-minus bands. Should produce an owner + timing launch checklist where EVERY row has an Owner, a T-minus timing, and a Done-when (not bare checkboxes). Should recommend building the owned audience before launch day and funneling rented reach into owned. Should compute a Launch Readiness Score with sub-scores and a GO/CONDITIONAL/NOT-READY verdict. Should schedule the dated milestones (not leave them in prose). Should name boundary handoffs (copywriting for the copy, ship for the GA cut, content-strategy for post-launch content).
assertions: Reads or checks for .agents/product-marketing-context.md
Classifies the launch TYPE before emitting tactics
Fills the ORB inventory table with the real numbers (500 email, 2k Twitter)
Forks the five phases mapped to the 6-week horizon with T-minus bands
Produces a checklist where every row has Owner + Timing + Done-when
Recommends audience building before launch and rented-into-owned funnel
Computes a Launch Readiness Score with a GO/CONDITIONAL/NOT-READY verdict
Schedules the dated milestones rather than leaving them in prose
Names boundary handoffs to copywriting / ship / content-strategy

evals.prompt: We want to launch on Product Hunt. Any tips? We've never done it before.
evals.expected_output: Should apply the verified 2026 PH mechanics, not stale advice. MUST cover: the fixed 12:01 AM PT launch time and the 24h race; the Featured vs All homepage/mobile curation (only Featured gets traffic, featuring is not guaranteed); the first-hours velocity dominance and the hidden-count first ~4 hours; weighted votes (established accounts beat day-0 accounts, so prime real PH-active supporters weeks ahead); the day-of-week tradeoff (Tue/Wed/Thu traffic vs Fri/Sat/Sun #1 odds); the ~6-month relaunch cooldown; and the 4 featured criteria. MUST state the HARD RULE never to ask for upvotes anywhere (shadowban/removal risk) and to ask for feedback/comments instead. Should present benchmarks only as a rough range with variance flagged, never a promise. Should flag that mechanics change and to re-verify at producthunt.com before a real launch. Should give a PH pre-launch checklist with owner + timing (aged account, primed supporters, sized assets, maker comment written ~48h ahead, 12:01 PT scheduled, zero vote-ask). Should set realistic expectations.
assertions: States the fixed 12:01 AM PT launch time and 24h race
Explains Featured vs All (only Featured gets homepage/mobile traffic, not guaranteed)
Covers first-hours velocity and weighted votes (prime aged supporters ahead)
Covers the day-of-week tradeoff (traffic vs #1 odds)
HARD RULE: never ask for upvotes anywhere; ask for feedback/comments instead
Presents benchmarks as a flagged range, not a promise
Flags that mechanics change and to re-verify at producthunt.com
Gives a PH pre-launch checklist with owner + timing per item

evals.prompt: we just shipped a major feature update. how should we announce it? it's not a full product launch, just a big new feature.
evals.expected_output: Should trigger on casual phrasing. Should classify this as a major-feature type (NOT run the full 5-phase product launch). Should apply the feature-announcement matrix: major update gets owned-list email + blog post + in-app announcement + social thread. Should give the decision rule for whether it deserves a mini-PH launch vs just a changelog (only if it is genuinely differentiated and clears the featuring bar standalone, and note it could dilute the ~6-month PH slot). Should keep every recommended action owner + timing shaped. Should hand the finished copy to copywriting and ongoing content to content-strategy.
assertions: Triggers on casual phrasing
Classifies as major-feature (not a full product launch)
Applies the feature-announcement matrix (email, blog, in-app, social)
Gives the mini-PH vs changelog decision rule
Scales tactics appropriately (not the full 5 phases)
Hands finished copy to copywriting / ongoing content to content-strategy

evals.prompt: Our launch flopped. We launched 3 weeks ago and only got 50 signups. We expected at least 500. What went wrong and what can we do now?
evals.expected_output: Should apply the flop-recovery playbook. MUST make the mandatory first move explicit: identify which channel produced the 50 signups you DID get and double down on THAT before adding any new channels. Should work the diagnosis tree: audience not primed pre-launch (the #1 cause, recovery is 30 days building the owned list before re-launching), wrong channel, weak positioning (rewrite the one-liner via copywriting, re-test), wrong ICP, bad timing, no Featured on PH. Should give a concrete 30-day recovery move per branch. Should leverage the early users (testimonials, referrals). Should NOT tell them to immediately add three new channels.
assertions: Mandatory first move: find which channel produced the signups and double down before adding new ones
Works the diagnosis tree across the failure branches
Names pre-launch audience-building as the #1 cause with a 30-day fix
Gives a concrete recovery move per branch
Suggests leveraging the early users for testimonials/referrals
Does not scatter into new channels while the working one is under-exploited

evals.prompt: How do we leverage partnerships and borrowed audiences for our launch? We don't have a big audience of our own.
evals.expected_output: Should focus on the Borrowed class of the ORB inventory. Should give specific borrowed tactics: podcast guest appearances, newsletter features, co-marketing, a reviewer/influencer who covers you, guest posts, community engagement, an affiliate/referral loop, audience-overlap tools (SparkToro, Listen Notes). MUST stress that every borrowed touch has to funnel into an OWNED capture (get the email) or the attention leaks away. Should note borrowed strategies take time and must start well before launch day. Should route the person-specific pitch to a partner/influencer to the outreach skill (draft-for-approval, not a blast). Should flag that thin owned means the first job is building an owned surface (a waitlist).
assertions: Focuses on the Borrowed class of the ORB inventory
Gives specific borrowed tactics (podcasts, newsletters, reviewer, affiliate, overlap tools)
Stresses funneling every borrowed touch into an owned capture
Notes borrowed strategies take time and start before launch day
Routes the person-specific pitch to the outreach skill (draft-for-approval)
Flags that thin owned means building a waitlist first

evals.prompt: Give me some creative marketing ideas to promote our product. We're bootstrapped and don't have a big budget.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a broad ongoing-marketing / ideation request, NOT a launch-moment task. Should defer to the boundary table: ongoing content and broad marketing ideation belong to content-strategy, not launch-strategy. Should NOT reference the nonexistent marketing-ideas skill. May offer a couple of launch-adjacent tactical ideas but should make clear that launch-strategy owns the launch MOMENT and sequence, and that content-strategy is the right skill for a broad brainstorm.
assertions: Recognizes this as a broad ongoing-marketing request, not a launch-moment task
Defers to content-strategy via the boundary table
Does NOT reference the nonexistent marketing-ideas skill
Does not attempt a full marketing brainstorm using launch patterns
Clarifies launch-strategy owns the launch moment and sequence

evals.prompt: we pushed a small bug-fix release and tweaked some copy. how do we launch it?
evals.expected_output: Should run the launch-type triage and the scale test, classify this as a minor-update, and SHORT-CIRCUIT: do NOT build a full launch plan for a minor update. Should recommend a changelog / release-notes entry plus an optional in-app toast, note it signals active development, and hand ongoing communication to content-strategy. Should explicitly say a minor update does not warrant the 5-phase launch or a Product Hunt push. Should not manufacture a launch where none is warranted.
assertions: Runs the type triage + scale test and classifies as minor-update
Short-circuits: does NOT build a full launch plan
Recommends a changelog / release-notes entry + optional in-app toast
Explicitly says it does not warrant the 5-phase launch or a PH push
Does not manufacture an unwarranted launch

evals.prompt: Draft our launch thread for X/Twitter and post it on our @aura0g account so it goes out at launch.
evals.expected_output: Should DRAFT the launch thread but NEVER auto-post it (the plan-and-draft-only gate; brand voice never auto-posts without Christopher's approval). Should apply the verified @aura0g X-launch constraints (referencing the x-launch-runbook): a single standalone tweet automates reliably but the native multi-tweet composer does not; a text thread works via the self-reply chain; the hero image goes on tweet 1 and reply-image attach is currently broken (image tweets manual-only); a non-Premium single post caps at 280 characters. Should defer all browser mechanics to the agent-browser skill (never Playwright MCP, never kill the live browser). Should keep the copy dash-free, emoji-free in brand voice, slop-free, and vote-ask-free. Should present the draft for Christopher to fire, not send it.
assertions: Drafts the thread but does NOT auto-post (draft-for-approval gate)
Applies the @aura0g constraints (single tweet reliable, thread via reply-chain, reply-image broken)
Notes the 280-char non-Premium cap
Defers browser mechanics to the agent-browser skill (never Playwright MCP)
Keeps copy dash-free, emoji-free, slop-free, and vote-ask-free
Presents the draft for Christopher to fire himself

evals.prompt: We're submitting our web3 app to a hackathon with a bracket: early rounds are judged by a jury, later rounds are a community vote. There's a code-lock date. How do we plan this launch?
evals.expected_output: Should classify this as a hackathon-submission type and follow the web3-hackathon playbook, NOT a Product Hunt launch. MUST identify the code-lock date as the hard T-0 for all build work (everything lands before it) and schedule it durably (Google Calendar for a weeks-out date). Should recognize the two-phase structure: win the jury rounds on MERIT (community reach does not bite yet), and community mobilization is the LATER vote-round lever. Should make the demo video the #1 jury artifact and build a judge-facing evidence package with on-chain receipts / code-true claims. Should warn NOT to frame opponents as weak in jury-facing material (backfires, DQ risk for misrepresentation) and NEVER to vote-rig in the vote round. Should reference the discovery sources (HackQuest, DoraHacks, Encode Club) if relevant.
assertions: Classifies as hackathon-submission, not a PH launch
Identifies the code-lock date as the hard T-0 and schedules it durably
Recognizes jury-first (merit) then community-vote-later structure
Makes the demo video the #1 jury artifact + a judge-facing evidence package
Warns against framing opponents as weak (backfire / DQ) and against vote-rigging
Community mobilization is the vote-round lever, not the jury lever

evals.prompt: Write our launch announcement: 'We're thrilled to announce our game-changing platform is finally here! The wait is over. Upvote us on Product Hunt!'
evals.expected_output: Should REJECT and rewrite this copy against the anti-slop gate and the hard rules. MUST flag and remove the banned slop ('we're thrilled to announce', 'game-changing', 'the wait is over'). MUST flag and REMOVE the vote-ask ('Upvote us on Product Hunt') as a HARD RULE violation that risks shadowban/removal, replacing it with a feedback/check-it-out phrasing. Should apply the specificity test (name the ONE concrete new thing and exactly who it is for). Should keep the rewrite dash-free and emoji-free. Should hand the finished, polished copy to the copywriting skill. Should NOT auto-send the announcement.
assertions: Rejects and rewrites the slop ('thrilled to announce', 'game-changing', 'the wait is over')
Flags and removes the vote-ask as a hard-rule violation (shadowban risk)
Applies the specificity test (the ONE new thing + who it is for)
Keeps the rewrite dash-free and emoji-free
Hands finished copy to the copywriting skill
Does not auto-send the announcement
